summary: Meta Platforms, Inc. (NASDAQ:META) is another frequently discussed stock in Cramer’s morning show . Cramer's recent remarks about the firm have focused on its AI efforts . He has repeatedly asserted that Meta platform is among the weakest in the market and is good for a few tasks .  AI stock is also a major beneficiary of Trump tariffs and onshoring, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock . delivering higher returns and have limited downside risk .

Technology and finance veterans with experience at Alphabet, Airbnb, Meta, LinkedIn, Netflix, and Pixar join to support Pattern's growth . Sue Taylor is currently an independent board member at Pure Storage and KLA . Ann Mather is the former chief financial officer of Pixar .  Ann Mather serves on the boards of Netflix and Bumble . Sue Taylor was the former CFO of Pixar Animation Studios . She was instrumental in shaping the company's financial strategy and growth .  Pattern’s board of directors will expand from five to seven members, with Sue Taylor and Ann Mather joining as independent directors . Since its founding in 2013, Pattern has profitably grown to more than 1,800 employees operating from 20 locations .

Meta Platforms (META,Financials) has hired four more AI researchers from OpenAI . The tech giant continues to aggressively expand its AI capabilities . The hiring spree highlights Metas accelerating push into high-end AI research .  This article was originally published by GuruFocus.com . Follow us on Twitter @dailymailonline.co.uk . Baird raised its price target to $740 from $635, citing growing momentum in messaging revenue and deeper AI integration across its platforms . Click-to-message ads are pacing toward a $1520 billion run rate, while WhatsApp ads could contribute another $10 billion over time .  Chinese e-commerce platform Temu has resumed ad buys on Meta platforms . European Commission has warned that Meta could face fines of up to 5% of daily revenue if its revised ad model fails to comply with the Digital Markets Act . Cantor Fitzgerald reaffirmed its Overweight rating, confident Meta can adapt without major disruption .
